item.headline: AI Research Engineer
item.about: I am a machine learning engineer and researcher with a PhD in computer science. As one of the first engineers at Axiom, I drive research in machine learning for mathematics, focusing on scaling and advancing AI models. Previously at FAIR, I co-developed LLM Compiler and Code World Model. LLM Compiler is a family of foundation models trained on compiler intermediate representations and assembly code to automate and improve optimization tasks. Code World Model is Meta’s 32B open-weights code LLM mid-trained on execution traces and agent–environment trajectories to model program state and improve code generation and reasoning. My academic and industry work includes multiple peer-reviewed and distinguished publications on large language models, compiler optimization, and machine learning for systems.

experience.position: Member of Technical Staff | AI for Mathematics
experience.description: As one of the first engineers on the team I contribute across the stack with a primary focus on developing and scaling AI models for mathematics.

Progressing in the field of AI research to boost the efficiency and functionality of computer systems, encompassing operating systems and compilers, by incorporating state-of-the-art machine learning methodologies.
Advancing AI research to enhance the effectiveness and performance of computer systems, including operating systems and compilers, through the integration of cutting-edge machine learning techniques.

The position is part of a project funded by Huawei Technologies and involves conducting research on how parallel programming patterns can be leveraged for adaptive workload scheduling on heterogeneous systems.
I am teaching computer science at the University of Edinburgh and engage in cutting edge research projects focusing on energy efficient mobile systems.

I am part of the Dividend project team which aims to develop energy efficient data centres by exploiting heterogeneous computing architecture. I implemented a server and client system which distributes the execution of OpenCL applications across available network resources. It makes use of OpenCL energy accounting on server side and a machine learning model on client side to find the most energy efficient and highest performing workload to execution device (CPU,GPU) mapping for the distributed application.

Thesis: User Experience Driven CPU Frequency Scaling on Mobile Devices Towards Better Energy Efficiency

developed a record and replay mechanism to deterministically replay realistic interactive Android workloads on the same/another mobile device with a micro second accuracy

developed a mechanism to automatically detect user interaction lag based on nonintrusive analysis of video output and device event queues with a speedup of 2700x compared to manual detection

identified energy saving potential of up to 27% whilst delivering a better user experience than the current standard Android CPU frequency governors

developed a reinforcement learning based CPU frequency governor for interactive Android workloads which achieved up to 22% energy savings compared to current mobile solutions whilst providing a fluid user experience

developed a simulator for interactive Android workloads with simulation speeds up to 100x faster than real time

Best Paper Award for publication at IISWC 2014 conference

- Android Display Stack
- Android Input Subsystem
- Linux Scheduler

In a close collaboration with the responsible team for the development and Linux integration of the big.little processor I had the chance to get an insight into development process and the research state of the team concerning the power efficiency of the big.little board.
- Designed and implemented an Android application to collect mobile workload statistics
- Designed and implemented a modular analysis tool chain to analyse collected workload data

experience.position: Student Assistant in the MeMo project Group
experience.description:
The MeMo project was aiming to develop a correctness model for graphical modeling and simulation
software such as Matlab, Simulink and Stateflow
- Designed and implemented a parser for Simulink-models
- Designed and implemented a prototype application to summarise model statistics

successfully presented the business idea to gain an EXIST Business Start-up Grant worth 63,000 € from the German government, office space and a collaboration with the international production house RCP in Munich

drove the agile project management for the Xbox 360 game Zeit2 which lead to successful publication by Ubisoft

implemented content loading pipeline, XML-interface to level editor, localisation, menu screens, game state transitions and various parts of the game logic

optimised the C# code base to cope with Xbox 360 garbage collection using object pooling and other techniques

experience.position: Software developer for automation systems and mobile game level designer
experience.description:
developed modules for decoding and processing signals based on the energy efficient EnOcean wireless protocol for embedded automation software which controls lights, window blinds and ventilation systems

developed a machine learning algorithm to control radiator valves by using features such as past and present room temperature, weather reports and room occupancies

education.description: User Experience Driven CPU Frequency Scaling On Mobile Devices Towards Better Energy Efficiency

Thesis: Design and Implementation of an Efficient Instruction Set Simulator for an Embedded Multi-Core Architecture

extended existing instruction set simulator code base to allow multi-core architecture simulation by synchronising and managing the code interpretation and execution on multiple core instances

final simulation speeds went up to 25,307 Mips on a 32-core x86 host for as many as 2048 simulated cores

Won the UK wide Science Engineering and Technology Student of the Year Award 2011 for Msc thesis

Published corresponding papers at the IC-SAMOS’11 conference and in the IJPP’12 journal
